title: Implement TelemetryMetric data model
description: Create the TelemetryMetric data model for aggregated telemetry metrics passed between the MetricsAggregator and the exporter pipeline. This model includes fields for metric type, timing, workspace/session/statement IDs, execution latency, result format, chunk details, poll metrics, and driver configuration snapshot.
design_context: TelemetryMetric is the data model for aggregated telemetry metrics. Fields include: MetricType (enum: Connection, Statement, Error), Timestamp, WorkspaceId, SessionId, StatementId, ExecutionLatencyMs, ResultFormat, ChunkCount, TotalBytesDownloaded, PollCount, PollLatencyMs, and DriverConfiguration (snapshot). JSON serialization must use snake_case property names to match the Databricks schema.
input_requirements: Design doc specifying all required fields
exit_criteria: All fields defined per design doc
JSON serialization uses snake_case property names
test_requirements: Test serialization produces valid JSON matching Databricks schema
Test null fields are omitted from serialization

title: Implement StatementTelemetryContext and TelemetrySessionContext
description: Create StatementTelemetryContext that holds all telemetry data for a single statement execution, populated inline by driver code. Also create TelemetrySessionContext (per-connection frozen session-level data). StatementTelemetryContext includes fields for session/statement IDs, auth type, system config, statement type, result format, timing (stopwatch-based), CloudFetch chunk details, and error info. Includes BuildTelemetryLog() method to construct the final proto.
design_context: StatementTelemetryContext is protocol-agnostic - works for both Thrift and SEA backends. Lives for the full duration of statement execution (ExecuteQueryAsync() to reader disposal). TelemetrySessionContext is created once at connection open and shared (read-only) with all statements. Uses a single Stopwatch for all timing to ensure consistency.
test_requirements: Test context creation and field population
Test BuildTelemetryLog() output
Test timing calculations from Stopwatch

title: Define ITelemetryClient interface
description: Create the ITelemetryClient interface that defines the contract for telemetry client operations. Interface includes Enqueue() for non-blocking event queueing, FlushAsync() for force-flushing pending events, and CloseAsync() for graceful shutdown with flush. One instance is shared per host via TelemetryClientManager.
design_context: ITelemetryClient receives telemetry events from multiple connections to the same host, batches them, and manages periodic flushing. Enqueue() is thread-safe and non-blocking. FlushAsync() is called when connection closes. CloseAsync() flushes pending events before disposing resources.
exit_criteria: Interface defines Enqueue(), FlushAsync(), and CloseAsync() methods
Documentation clearly describes thread-safety and lifecycle

title: Implement TelemetryClientManager with reference counting
description: Create singleton TelemetryClientManager that manages one telemetry client per host with thread-safe reference counting. Implements GetOrCreateClient() which creates new client or increments ref count, and ReleaseClientAsync() which decrements ref count and closes/removes client when count reaches zero. Uses ConcurrentDictionary<string, TelemetryClientHolder> for thread safety. Client is flushed before removal on last reference release.
design_context: TelemetryClientManager prevents rate limiting by sharing clients across concurrent connections to the same host (important for large customers like Celonis who open many parallel connections). Reference counting ensures proper cleanup. Thread-safe for concurrent access from multiple connections.
exit_criteria: Thread-safe reference counting using Interlocked operations
Client flushed on last reference release
test_requirements: Test new host creates client with RefCount=1
Test existing host returns same client and increments RefCount
Test last reference release closes and removes client
Test multiple references keep client active
Test concurrent calls from 10 threads create single client instance

title: Implement CircuitBreakerManager
description: Create singleton CircuitBreakerManager that manages circuit breakers per host. Simple wrapper over ConcurrentDictionary to ensure one circuit breaker instance per host. GetCircuitBreaker(host) returns existing or creates new CircuitBreaker for host. Uses default config from CircuitBreakerConfig (5 failures, 1 min timeout, 2 successes to close).
design_context: CircuitBreakerManager ensures one circuit breaker per host for isolation. The existing CircuitBreaker implementation is already complete (WI-3.1), this task just adds the manager singleton to coordinate per-host instances.
exit_criteria: One circuit breaker per host guaranteed
test_requirements: Test new host creates new CircuitBreaker
Test same host returns same CircuitBreaker instance
Test different hosts get different CircuitBreaker instances

title: Implement CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter wrapper
description: Create CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter that wraps ITelemetryExporter with circuit breaker protection. Implements ITelemetryExporter and delegates to CircuitBreakerManager.GetCircuitBreaker(host) for per-host isolation. When circuit is open, events are silently dropped (logged at DEBUG level). When closed, events pass through to inner exporter. Inner exporter failures are tracked by circuit breaker.
design_context: CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter protects against failing telemetry endpoint (5xx errors, timeouts, network failures). Circuit breaker MUST see exceptions before they are swallowed, otherwise it won't work. After threshold failures, circuit opens and events are dropped to prevent wasting resources. Auto-recovers when endpoint becomes healthy.
exit_criteria: Silent drop when circuit open (DEBUG log only)
test_requirements: Test circuit closed allows metrics export
Test circuit open drops metrics without exception
Test inner exporter failure increments circuit breaker failure count

title: Implement MetricsAggregator for Activity-based collection
description: Create MetricsAggregator that aggregates Activity data by statement_id and coordinates export. Processes activities via ProcessActivity(), aggregates statement events by statement_id in ConcurrentDictionary, emits connection events immediately, handles terminal vs retryable errors appropriately, and flushes batches when threshold or time interval reached. Uses TelemetryTagRegistry to filter tags. All exceptions swallowed (logged at TRACE level).
design_context: MetricsAggregator is the core orchestration component. Connection events emitted immediately (no aggregation). Statement events aggregated by statement_id. Error events: terminal errors flush immediately, retryable errors buffer until statement complete. CompleteStatement(statementId) emits aggregated metric. FlushAsync() flushes when batch size or time interval reached. Tag filtering prevents sensitive data export.
exit_criteria: Immediate emit for connection and terminal error events
Batch flush on threshold and time interval
Tag filtering via TelemetryTagRegistry prevents sensitive data export
test_requirements: Test connection activity emits immediately
Test statement activities aggregate by statement_id
Test CompleteStatement emits metric
Test batch size threshold triggers flush
Test time interval triggers flush
Test terminal exception flushes immediately
Test retryable exception buffers until complete
Test exceptions are swallowed
Test tag filtering works

title: Implement DatabricksActivityListener
description: Create DatabricksActivityListener that listens to System.Diagnostics.Activity events from 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver' ActivitySource and delegates to MetricsAggregator. Sample callback respects feature flag (returns AllDataAndRecorded or None). ActivityStopped callback delegates to MetricsAggregator.ProcessActivity(). All callbacks wrapped in try-catch. StopAsync() flushes pending metrics and disposes listener. Implements IDisposable.
design_context: DatabricksActivityListener bridges Activity infrastructure to telemetry pipeline. Filters to only Databricks ActivitySource. Sample callback controls whether activities are recorded based on feature flag. ActivityStopped is where telemetry processing happens. Never throws to Activity infrastructure (critical for not breaking tracing).
exit_criteria: Respects feature flag via Sample callback
test_requirements: Test ShouldListenTo returns true for Databricks source
Test ActivityStopped calls MetricsAggregator.ProcessActivity
Test exceptions in callbacks are swallowed
Test feature flag disabled returns None
Test feature flag enabled returns AllDataAndRecorded
Test StopAsync flushes and disposes resources

title: Implement TelemetryClient coordinating listener/aggregator/exporter
description: Create TelemetryClient that coordinates the listener, aggregator, and exporter into a cohesive lifecycle. This is the public-facing API that DatabricksConnection interacts with. Constructor initializes: DatabricksTelemetryExporter → CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter → MetricsAggregator → DatabricksActivityListener. ExportAsync() delegates to exporter. CloseAsync() flushes pending metrics, cancels background tasks, disposes resources. All exceptions swallowed in CloseAsync().
design_context: TelemetryClient is the main entry point that brings together all telemetry components. Manages full pipeline lifecycle: listener captures activities, aggregator processes them, exporter sends to Databricks. Clean shutdown is critical - must flush before closing to avoid losing telemetry data.
test_requirements: Test constructor initializes all components correctly
Test ExportAsync delegates to exporter
Test CloseAsync flushes pending metrics
Test CloseAsync cancels background tasks
Test exceptions in CloseAsync are swallowed

title: Add telemetry-specific tags to driver Activities
description: Enhance existing driver Activities with telemetry-specific tags so MetricsAggregator can extract meaningful metrics. Add tags to DatabricksStatement.cs (result.format, result.chunk_count, result.bytes_downloaded, statement.type), DatabricksOperationStatusPoller (poll.count, poll.latency_ms, time_to_first_byte_ms), DatabricksConnection.cs (driver.version, driver.os, driver.runtime, feature.cloudfetch, feature.lz4), CloudFetch reader classes (compression.enabled, row_count). Use names from TagDefinitions registry. Ensure no sensitive data in tags.
design_context: Activity tags are the source data for telemetry collection. Tags added at key lifecycle points during statement execution. All tag names come from TagDefinitions registry. Tags must not contain PII or sensitive data - verified against TelemetryTagRegistry. These tags enable rich metrics like chunk counts, compression usage, poll statistics.
output_deliverables: Modified CloudFetch readers with compression/row tags
Unit test additions to existing test files
exit_criteria: All specified tags added to correct Activities
Tags use names from TagDefinitions registry
No sensitive data in tags (verified against TelemetryTagRegistry)
test_requirements: Test statement activity has result.format tag
Test statement activity has result.chunk_count tag
Test connection activity has driver.version tag
Test connection activity has feature flags tags

title: Wire telemetry into DatabricksConnection lifecycle
description: Integrate telemetry components into DatabricksConnection lifecycle. In OpenAsync(): check feature flag via FeatureFlagCache, if enabled call TelemetryClientManager.GetOrCreateClient(), start activity listener. In Dispose(): flush pending metrics, TelemetryClientManager.ReleaseClientAsync(), FeatureFlagCache.ReleaseContext(). Merge feature flags into Properties dictionary at initialization (priority: User properties > Feature flags > Defaults). Handle errors gracefully - telemetry failures must not impact connection behavior.
design_context: DatabricksConnection integration is the critical wiring that connects all telemetry components to the driver. Telemetry initialized after successful session creation. Feature flag check gates initialization. Clean shutdown sequence: flush → release client → release feature flags. Priority merge ensures user settings always win over server flags. Telemetry is completely non-blocking.
exit_criteria: Telemetry initialized after successful session creation
Feature flag check gates telemetry initialization
Clean shutdown: flush → release client → release feature flags
No impact on connection behavior when telemetry fails
test_requirements: Test connection with telemetry enabled calls GetOrCreateClient
Test feature flag disabled prevents telemetry initialization
Test connection dispose calls ReleaseClientAsync
Test pending metrics are flushed before client release

title: Implement comprehensive E2E telemetry tests
description: Create comprehensive end-to-end tests validating full telemetry flow against live Databricks environment. Tests cover: connection events, statement events, CloudFetch chunk metrics, error events, feature flag disabled scenario, multiple connections sharing client, circuit breaker behavior, and graceful shutdown with flush. Tests use SkippableFact for cases where credentials aren't configured. Validate performance overhead < 1%, zero exceptions propagated, and circuit breaker isolation works correctly.
design_context: E2E tests validate the complete telemetry pipeline in a real environment. Critical for verifying: telemetry data flows end-to-end, multiple connections share same client (preventing rate limiting), circuit breaker protects against failing endpoints, graceful shutdown flushes all pending events, and performance overhead is acceptable. Tests must be skippable when no credentials configured.
exit_criteria: All E2E tests pass against live Databricks environment
Tests are skippable using SkippableFact when no credentials
Zero exceptions propagated to driver operations
Circuit breaker correctly isolates failing endpoints
test_requirements: Test connection event exported with driver config
Test statement event with execution latency
Test large query exports chunk metrics
Test invalid SQL exports error event
Test feature flag off prevents export
Test 3 connections to same host share single client
Test circuit breaker drops events after threshold failures
Test close connection flushes all pending events
